# Agent Catalog — knowledge

## decision
- [3.5 catalog field set finalized](/features/agent-catalog/knowledge/decision-agent-catalog-35-field-set.md) — Field-finalization 2026-07-02 (+ spec-doc comments) — drop agent-type; optional "tested models"; deploy button detail-page only; capabilities cut; multi-value "communication protocol"; RH-curated labels/logo via agent.yaml.
- [Catalog deploy restricted to platform-built (supported) images](/features/agent-catalog/knowledge/decision-agent-catalog-deploy-supported-images-only.md) — 2026-06-01 packaging meeting — the deploy MVP only deploys images Red Hat builds (Mode 2); Mode 1 bring-your-own is documentation-only at first; proprietary harnesses ship as Containerfiles.
- [No deploy button in 3.5 — catalog links out to GitHub](/features/agent-catalog/knowledge/decision-agent-catalog-no-deploy-35.md) — AgentDev sync 2026-07-09 — 3.5 ships a read-only catalog (no deploy, no agent-card discovery, no rich detail page); agents table discovers AgentSandbox CRs; deployment moves to 3.6.
- [Harness kits in scope for 3.5 agent catalog](/features/agent-catalog/knowledge/decision-harness-kits-in-scope-35-catalog.md) — Adel Zaalouk confirmed (~2026-07-11) that all agent kits already in the agentic-starter-kits repo are in scope for 3.5 catalog -- including harness kits (OpenCode, Claude Code, OpenClaw, etc.), not just template kits.
- [Maintained catalog images are "supported", not "validated"](/features/agent-catalog/knowledge/decision-supported-not-validated-images.md) — 2026-07-10 — in RHOAI "validated" already means vendor-provided images Red Hat certifies but the vendor maintains (NVIDIA Triton); catalog images Red Hat maintains are "supported".

## fact
- [Agent catalog 3.5 filter_options real data shape](/features/agent-catalog/knowledge/fact-agent-catalog-35-filter-response.md) — Real filter_options endpoint response as of ~2026-07-11 -- 14 agents (templates + harnesses), 11 frameworks, 2 deployment models (config-driven, flow-import), 16 labels; harness kits (OpenCode, Claude Code, OpenClaw) appear alongside template kits.
- [Agent Catalog 3.5 — scope and delivery state](/features/agent-catalog/knowledge/fact-agent-catalog-35-scope.md) — 3.5 = read-only starter-kit catalog + read-only deployments view; YAML catalog source (disconnected mandatory); no admin UI; OpenAPI merged 2026-07-03; ownership map across AI Hub/Razzmatazz/AgentDev/AgentOps.
- [Agent Catalog 3.6 — supported images direction](/features/agent-catalog/knowledge/fact-agent-catalog-36-supported-images.md) — 3.6 deployment = OpenShell Go SDK + supported harness images (OpenCode/Hermes favored; licensing flags on Claude Code/Antigravity); AIPCC base images ADR; staleness-vs-disconnected tension; skills "tested with models".
- [Agent Catalog — market position](/features/agent-catalog/knowledge/fact-agent-catalog-market-position.md) — The industry converged on the same template→deployable→registry split we're sequencing (validating the design, 12-18mo late); nobody ships supported self-run harness images; IBM is the on-prem coopetitor; registries are now monetized.
- [Agent Catalog — overview](/features/agent-catalog/knowledge/fact-agent-catalog-overview.md) — The agent storefront in RHOAI AI Hub — RH-curated starter-kit templates in 3.5 (link-out, no deploy), deployment via supported images in 3.6 EA1; Kubeflow hub, sibling of the MCP and Model catalogs.
- [Starter kit deploy contract (now 3.6)](/features/agent-catalog/knowledge/fact-agent-catalog-starter-kits.md) — The container contract for deployable starter kits — port 8000, A2A agent-card endpoint, non-root, env-var config; written for the 3.5 deploy wizard, now governs the 3.6 deploy flow.
- [Upstream agent-catalog schema (kubeflow/hub) and the product divergence](/features/agent-catalog/knowledge/fact-agent-catalog-upstream-schema.md) — PR
- [Agent deployment modes (Mode 1 / Mode 2) and the declarative harness](/features/agent-catalog/knowledge/fact-agent-deployment-modes.md) — Mode 1 = bring-your-own image + sidecars by contract; Mode 2 = platform-owned deploy of supported harness images with declarative binding to the platform registries; agentic API surface underneath.
- [Ann Marie's deploy-from-catalog POC proposal](/features/agent-catalog/knowledge/fact-ann-marie-poc-proposal.md) — POC plan — pre-built agent image + MLflow skill bundle (install-bundle from RFC PR
- [Catalog deploy wizard — dashboard-owned, BFF path](/features/agent-catalog/knowledge/fact-catalog-deploy-wizard-ownership.md) — The deploy wizard is dashboard-owned (Gage Krumbach); catalog-to-deploy uses extensions + BFF communication to prefill the wizard; env vars settable at deploy time; MCP/skills not in UX designs yet (discovery only).
- [deploymentModel is a customProperty with a parsing gap](/features/agent-catalog/knowledge/fact-deployment-model-metadata-gap.md) — deploymentModel is a kubeflow/hub customProperty (not typed) with a parsing gap -- agent.yaml top-level key silently ignored by the Go struct; only 2/15 kits set it (langflow flow-import, openclaw config-driven); config-driven and flow-import are fundamentally different deploy mechanisms (container vs platform-instance import).
- [Enterprise requirements on supported images (air-gap, security, licensing)](/features/agent-catalog/knowledge/fact-supported-images-enterprise-requirements.md) — Air-gapped segments prohibit runtime fetch outright (staleness debate resolves toward disconnected); supported images inherit Health Index/CVE/Konflux obligations; Claude Code redistribution is license-prohibited, Codex/Goose are Apache-2.0; EU AI Act transparency lands 2026-08-02.

## person
- [Aakanksha Duggal](/features/agent-catalog/knowledge/person-aakanksha-duggal.md) — AgentDev — builds and validates the starter-kit and harness images near-term (OpenClaw on OpenShift 4.19, UBI image track); party to the 2026-07-09 no-deploy call.
- [Alessio Pragliola](/features/agent-catalog/knowledge/person-alessio-pragliola.md) — Agent Catalog backend owner (RHAISTRAT-1740, kubeflow/hub) — assigned 2026-06-04; owns the 3.5 spec-finalization doc; landed the OpenAPI spec.
- [Jehlum Vitasta Pandit](/features/agent-catalog/knowledge/person-jehlum-vitasta-pandit.md) — Starter kits + 3.6 harness onboarding — owns the harness-candidates list and the platform-skills bootstrap proposal; liaison to the AI Quick Starts team.
- [Yabbes Rajan](/features/agent-catalog/knowledge/person-yabbes-rajan.md) — UX designer leading the Agent Catalog design — owns the catalog/detail screens and prototype; drove the 3.5 field walkthrough.

## question
- [Will AIPCC base images fit the catalog's needs?](/features/agent-catalog/knowledge/question-agent-catalog-aipcc-base-images-fit.md) — AIPCC is building agent-runtime base images for 3.6 (GitLab ADR MR 224), but catalog contents change constantly and AIPCC is out of the loop; Daniele Zonca to comment on the ADR.
- [Does the catalog enter the agentic-framework support space?](/features/agent-catalog/knowledge/question-agent-catalog-framework-support-boundary.md) — Shipping images embedding LangChain/LangGraph/CrewAI drags the team into CVE/security maintenance explicitly outside original scope; purpose-built agents deferred.
- [Off-the-shelf harness integration with the agent catalog playground](/features/agent-catalog/knowledge/question-agent-catalog-harness-playground-integration.md) — Full chat-interface integration for harnesses like OpenCode/Claude Code needs session management, human-in-the-loop, and tool approval — not 3.5 scope.
- [Who owns packaging and maintaining supported images long-term?](/features/agent-catalog/knowledge/question-agent-catalog-ownership-packaging.md) — AgentDev (Aakanksha Duggal) builds/validates harness and starter-kit images near-term; long-term ownership, support boundaries, and QE are unassigned — open since the 2026-06-01 packaging meeting.
- [Should the agent catalog standardize on one protocol or support several?](/features/agent-catalog/knowledge/question-agent-catalog-protocol-diversity.md) — Open call on whether starter-kit harnesses should all translate to A2A, or support multiple protocols with labels.
- [Where do agent starter kits end and AI Quick Starts begin?](/features/agent-catalog/knowledge/question-agent-catalog-quickstarts-overlap.md) — Daniele Zonca sees starter kits risking "glorified quick starts"; Jehlum - the quick-starts team declined day-zero enablement, which is why starter kits exist; boundary unresolved.
- [Must deployments register — and does Deploy become Register?](/features/agent-catalog/knowledge/question-agent-catalog-register-vs-deploy.md) — Platform stance on catalog-registry-deployments flow unresolved (ODH ADR
- [Which harnesses ship as supported images in 3.6?](/features/agent-catalog/knowledge/question-agent-catalog-supported-harness-set.md) — Candidate set open (OpenCode/Hermes favored; Claude Code/Antigravity need licensing; OpenCode self-update vs disconnected); no committed list or rotation policy yet.
- [Should 3.6 EA1 deploy support flow-import agents or descope to config-driven only?](/features/agent-catalog/knowledge/question-flow-import-deploy-scope.md) — flow-import agents (Langflow) require a completely different deploy backend (provision/locate a platform instance + import flow JSON) alongside the OpenShell container path -- should 3.6 EA1 support both or descope?
- [Skills/MCP configuration at deploy time — not in UX designs](/features/agent-catalog/knowledge/question-skills-at-deploy-time.md) — MCP and skills set at runtime are not part of the deploy wizard UX designs (Gage, 2026-07-11); only agent-card skill/MCP discovery exists. No mechanism to say "use this skill for my harness" at deploy time.
